status: paper-facing internal audit; no new CFD results
main_findings: Mesh-derived geometry should be the authoritative geometry contract; schematic probe CSV labels are not sufficient for closure derivation.
Section-mean total pressure exists, but variable-density buoyancy terms must be separated before heated or cooled non-isothermal leg pressure gradients are interpreted as pure friction.
The current HTC/UA'/Nu extractor has the correct enthalpy-flux bulk-temperature structure, but publication tables still need explicit heat-flux sign convention and uncertainty labeling.
Current f(Re) and Nu(Re) surfaces are narrow laminar calibrations, not transferable correlations.
Reusable thesis workflows should standardize closure observations, model-form specs, and model-error summaries.
